q: The elements having low ionisation energy easily lose electrons, resulting in the formation of the ________.
The higher the value of electronegativity, the ________ is the atom's attraction to electrons.
The ________ bond involves electron transfer between two atoms.
Ionic bonding depends on the difference in ________ between two atoms.
Which condition leads to the formation of an ionic bond?
a: Between elements with a large electronegativity difference.

q: Which type of elements typically form ionic bonds?
In NaCl, the sodium atom (Na) forms a ________ by losing an electron.
An ionic bond is formed by ________ of electrons between two atoms.
The atomic number of calcium is 20. Calcium chloride is an ionic compound. Which of the following statements is true for the formation of calcium chloride?
Which forces hold the cations and anions together in ionic compounds?
The coordination number of Na+ and Cl- in sodium chloride crystal is ________.
Covalent bonds are formed when electrons are ________.
Which molecule contains a triple covalent bond?
How many electrons are shared in a single covalent bond?
The optimum distance between two nuclei is called ________.
Which of the following molecules has a double covalent bond?
How many total valence electrons participate in a triple bond?
Which pair of elements is most likely to form a covalent bond?
Which of the following molecules has a triple bond?
A dative bond is formed by ________.
A coordinate bond is a special type of ________.
Which theory is used to predict the shapes of covalent molecules?
What is the shape of a molecule with two bonding pairs and no lone pairs?
The angle between two bonds in a linear molecule is ________.
What is the shape of a molecule with three bonding pairs and no lone pairs?
Which molecule has a tetrahedral shape?
What is the shape of a molecule with two bonding pairs and two lone pairs?
According to VSEPR theory, the molecular shape of beryllium chloride (BeCl2) is ________.
According to VSEPR theory, the bond angle of BF3 is ________.
The shape of the ammonia molecule is ________.
In diamond, each carbon atom is covalently bonded to ________ other carbon atoms.
Which of the following is NOT an allotrope of carbon?
Which structure has a giant covalent network?
Silicon dioxide does not conduct electricity because it has ________.
In silica, each silicon atom is bonded to ________ oxygen atoms.
The shape of water molecule (H2O) is ________.
In a non-polar covalent bond, electrons are shared ________.
A polar covalent bond occurs when there is a difference in ________ between the atoms.
Which molecule represents a nonpolar covalent bond?
The measure of polarity in a molecule is its ________.
The ________ end of a dipole is the atom with the highest electronegativity.
Which of the following is a non-polar molecule due to its linear shape?
What type of bond involves unequal sharing of electrons?
Intermolecular forces are ________ than intramolecular forces.
Which of the following is NOT an intermolecular force?
London dispersion forces occur between ________ molecules.
Dipole-dipole interactions occur between ________ molecules.
Which intermolecular force is the strongest?
Hydrogen bonding can occur when hydrogen is bonded to ________.
London dispersion force is also called ________.
Which forces are responsible for holding H2O molecules together in ice?
The metallic bond is the attraction between positive metal ions and ________.
Which model is used to describe metallic bonding?
Metals are good conductors of electricity because of ________.
What is true about metallic structures?
a: They are poor conductors of heat.

q: The principal quantum number indicates ________.
The number of sublevels in a principal energy level is equal to ________.
The maximum number of electrons that can occupy a main energy level is ________.
Which subshell has a spherical shape?
Which subshell has a dumbbell shape?
How many orbitals are there in a p-subshell?
How many orbitals are there in a d-subshell?
The maximum number of electrons that can occupy an orbital is ________.
The maximum number of electrons that can occupy a d-subshell is ________.
The ________ states that electrons occupy the lowest energy orbital first.
The ________ states that no two electrons in an atom can have the same four quantum numbers.
The ________ states that every orbital in a subshell is singly occupied before any orbital is doubly occupied.
Electrons in the outermost shell are called ________.
The octet rule states that atoms tend to gain, lose, or share electrons until they have ________ valence electrons.
Lewis symbols use dots to represent ________.
The atomic number of chlorine is 17. How many valence electrons does it have?
The attractive forces that hold atoms together in compounds are called ________.
Which of the following is NOT a primary type of chemical bond?
Electronegativity is the ability of an atom to ________.
The amount of energy released when an electron is added to a gaseous atom is ________.
The amount of energy required to remove an electron from a gaseous atom is ________.
In an endothermic reaction, the total enthalpy of the products is ________ the total enthalpy of the reactants.
The symbol ΔH for an exothermic reaction is ________.
Which equation represents an exothermic reaction?
a: C(s) + O2(g) -> CO2(g) ΔH = -393.5 kJ mol-1
CaCO3(s) -> CaO(s) + CO2(g) ΔH = +178 kJ mol-1

q: Standard conditions for enthalpy changes are ________.
The enthalpy change of formation is the enthalpy change when ________ of a substance is formed from its elements under standard conditions.
The standard enthalpy change of formation of any element in its standard state is ________.
The enthalpy change of combustion is always ________.
Which type of reaction always releases heat?
The enthalpy change of neutralization involves the formation of ________.
The enthalpy change of solution can be ________.
Hess's Law is based on the principle of conservation of ________.
According to Hess's Law, the enthalpy change of a reaction is ________ of the pathway taken.
Which formula represents Hess's Law?
Bond energy is the energy required to ________ a specific covalent bond in one mole of gaseous molecules.
Bond breaking is an ________ process.
Bond forming is an ________ process.
The enthalpy change of a reaction can be estimated using ________.
If the energy released during bond formation is greater than the energy required for bond breaking, the overall reaction is ________.
A calorimeter is used to measure ________.
The specific heat capacity of a substance is the amount of heat required to raise the temperature of ________ of the substance by 1 K or 1°C.
The formula to calculate heat transfer (q) is ________.
In the formula q = m c ΔT, 'c' represents ________.
What is the specific heat capacity of water?
A coffee-cup calorimeter operates at constant ________.
